## Scale for colour is already present.
## Adding another scale for colour, which will replace the existing scale.
p22 <- plot(eff_int_hyb) + 
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 1:4, labels = my_labels) + 
  scale_color_manual(values = my_colors) + 
  labs(
    title = "Option 2: Screen Time",
    x = "Condition", 
    y = "Estimated Time (Seconds)",
    colour = "Domain"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
## Scale for x is already present.
## Adding another scale for x, which will replace the existing scale.
## Scale for colour is already present.
## Adding another scale for colour, which will replace the existing scale.
combined_plot <- p11 + p22 + 
  plot_layout(guides = "collect") + 
  plot_annotation(
    title = "Predicted Translation Time: Comparison of Imputation Scenarios",
    subtitle = "Values back-transformed from Log to Seconds",
    tag_levels = 'a', 
    tag_prefix = '(',
    tag_suffix = ')'
  ) & 
  theme(
    legend.position = "right",
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold")
  )

combined_plot

Model Building - Keystrokes

Analysis of outcome variable distribution

ggplot(data, aes(x = keystrokes)) +
  geom_histogram(aes(y = ..density..),
                 bins = 30,
                 fill = "lightblue",
                 color = "black") +
  geom_density(color = "red", size = 1) +
  labs(title = "Distribution of Keystrokes",
       x = "Keystrokes",
       y = "Density")

Tweedie GLMM

The Tweedie distribution is a member of the Exponential Dispersion Family. Here, it is specifically a Compound Poisson-Gamma distribution.

It acts as a single mathematical framework that handles two processes at once: 1) The Discrete Part (Poisson) models the probability of an event occurring (e.g., whether the translator chooses to edit the sentence or leave it at 0 keystrokes); The Continuous Part (Gamma) models the magnitude of the event (e.g., if they do edit, how many keystrokes are required)

full_model <- glmmTMB(keystrokes ~ condition * domain + num_characters + num_minor + num_major + MT_Quality + Difficulty + 
                   (1 | PET) + (1 | text_name) + (1 | Sent_id), 
                 family = tweedie(), data = data, REML = FALSE)
## Warning in (function (start, objective, gradient = NULL, hessian = NULL, :
## NA/NaN function evaluation
summary(full_model)
##  Family: tweedie  ( log )
## Formula:          
## keystrokes ~ condition * domain + num_characters + num_minor +  
##     num_major + MT_Quality + Difficulty + (1 | PET) + (1 | text_name) +  
##     (1 | Sent_id)
## Data: data
## 
##       AIC       BIC    logLik -2*log(L)  df.resid 
##    4540.6    4616.3   -2252.3    4504.6       478 
## 
## Random effects:
## 
## Conditional model:
##  Groups    Name        Variance Std.Dev.
##  PET       (Intercept) 0.22448  0.4738  
##  text_name (Intercept) 0.07371  0.2715  
##  Sent_id   (Intercept) 0.01331  0.1154  
## Number of obs: 496, groups:  PET, 8; text_name, 8; Sent_id, 10
## 
## Dispersion parameter for tweedie family (): 5.07 
## 
## Conditional model:
##                         Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)    
## (Intercept)            3.1923025  0.3131078  10.196  < 2e-16 ***
## condition2            -0.2059139  0.1724809  -1.194 0.232542    
## condition3            -0.0322123  0.1629450  -0.198 0.843289    
## condition4            -0.1364385  0.1593411  -0.856 0.391850    
## domainnews            -0.0585156  0.2463233  -0.238 0.812226    
## num_characters         0.0035341  0.0004367   8.092 5.85e-16 ***
## num_minor              0.0615679  0.0238404   2.583 0.009809 ** 
## num_major              0.0762211  0.0462809   1.647 0.099574 .  
## MT_Quality            -0.2096219  0.0622165  -3.369 0.000754 ***
## Difficulty             0.0871202  0.0546948   1.593 0.111196    
## condition2:domainnews  0.2543400  0.2077050   1.225 0.220754    
## condition3:domainnews  0.1797527  0.2036987   0.882 0.377537    
## condition4:domainnews  0.0474774  0.2064133   0.230 0.818083    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
full_model$sdr$pdHess
## [1] TRUE
res <- simulateResiduals(full_model)
plot(res)

# A more simplified model
refined_model <- glmmTMB(keystrokes ~ condition * domain + 
                             num_characters + num_minor + num_major + 
                             MT_Quality + Difficulty + 
                             (1 | PET) + (1 | text_name), 
                           family = tweedie(), 
                           data = data)
## Warning in (function (start, objective, gradient = NULL, hessian = NULL, :
## NA/NaN function evaluation
summary(refined_model)
##  Family: tweedie  ( log )
## Formula:          
## keystrokes ~ condition * domain + num_characters + num_minor +  
##     num_major + MT_Quality + Difficulty + (1 | PET) + (1 | text_name)
## Data: data
## 
##       AIC       BIC    logLik -2*log(L)  df.resid 
##    4541.8    4613.3   -2253.9    4507.8       479 
## 
## Random effects:
## 
## Conditional model:
##  Groups    Name        Variance Std.Dev.
##  PET       (Intercept) 0.2224   0.4716  
##  text_name (Intercept) 0.0716   0.2676  
## Number of obs: 496, groups:  PET, 8; text_name, 8
## 
## Dispersion parameter for tweedie family (): 5.08 
## 
## Conditional model:
##                         Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)    
## (Intercept)            3.2173385  0.3079667  10.447  < 2e-16 ***
## condition2            -0.2090329  0.1728145  -1.210 0.226440    
## condition3            -0.0284707  0.1636996  -0.174 0.861928    
## condition4            -0.1322924  0.1602395  -0.826 0.409036    
## domainnews            -0.0433218  0.2445704  -0.177 0.859403    
## num_characters         0.0033949  0.0003983   8.525  < 2e-16 ***
## num_minor              0.0587039  0.0233259   2.517 0.011847 *  
## num_major              0.0970654  0.0447759   2.168 0.030174 *  
## MT_Quality            -0.2068991  0.0626836  -3.301 0.000964 ***
## Difficulty             0.0846716  0.0550203   1.539 0.123825    
## condition2:domainnews  0.2405202  0.2089792   1.151 0.249762    
## condition3:domainnews  0.1911759  0.2051732   0.932 0.351451    
## condition4:domainnews  0.0493353  0.2081055   0.237 0.812604    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

1. Scale variables to get numerical stability and Check stability

final_recovery_model <- glmmTMB(
  keystrokes ~ condition * domain + 
    scale(num_characters) + 
    scale(num_minor) + 
    scale(num_major) + 
    scale(MT_Quality) + 
    scale(Difficulty) + 
    (1 | PET) +
    (1 | text_name / Sent_id),       
  family = tweedie(), 
  data = data
)

final_recovery_model$sdr$pdHess
## [1] TRUE
res <- simulateResiduals(final_recovery_model)
plot(res)

summary(final_recovery_model)
##  Family: tweedie  ( log )
## Formula:          
## keystrokes ~ condition * domain + scale(num_characters) + scale(num_minor) +  
##     scale(num_major) + scale(MT_Quality) + scale(Difficulty) +  
##     (1 | PET) + (1 | text_name/Sent_id)
## Data: data
## 
##       AIC       BIC    logLik -2*log(L)  df.resid 
##    4487.6    4563.3   -2225.8    4451.6       478 
## 
## Random effects:
## 
## Conditional model:
##  Groups            Name        Variance Std.Dev.
##  PET               (Intercept) 0.24305  0.4930  
##  Sent_id:text_name (Intercept) 0.19266  0.4389  
##  text_name         (Intercept) 0.06014  0.2452  
## Number of obs: 496, groups:  PET, 8; Sent_id:text_name, 62; text_name, 8
## 
## Dispersion parameter for tweedie family ():  4.7 
## 
## Conditional model:
##                       Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)    
## (Intercept)            3.51186    0.25558  13.741  < 2e-16 ***
## condition2            -0.10173    0.17166  -0.593 0.553429    
## condition3             0.04825    0.15716   0.307 0.758862    
## condition4            -0.05910    0.15299  -0.386 0.699265    
## domainnews            -0.07359    0.25094  -0.293 0.769312    
## scale(num_characters)  0.36750    0.06919   5.311 1.09e-07 ***
## scale(num_minor)       0.05409    0.05554   0.974 0.330083    
## scale(num_major)       0.06744    0.04258   1.584 0.113257    
## scale(MT_Quality)     -0.16056    0.04422  -3.631 0.000282 ***
## scale(Difficulty)      0.11245    0.05597   2.009 0.044529 *  
## condition2:domainnews  0.19956    0.19111   1.044 0.296385    
## condition3:domainnews  0.19469    0.18488   1.053 0.292329    
## condition4:domainnews  0.08572    0.18722   0.458 0.647066    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

Visualising random intercepts

plot_model(final_recovery_model, type = "re")
## [[1]]

## 
## [[2]]

## 
## [[3]]

Simplifying the model to make residuals homoschedastic

# Creating the simplified version: no interaction term
reduced_model <- update(final_recovery_model, . ~ . - condition:domain)

# Compare them
anova(final_recovery_model, reduced_model)
## Data: data
## Models:
## reduced_model: keystrokes ~ condition + domain + scale(num_characters) + scale(num_minor) + , zi=~0, disp=~1
## reduced_model:     scale(num_major) + scale(MT_Quality) + scale(Difficulty) + , zi=~0, disp=~1
## reduced_model:     (1 | PET) + (1 | text_name/Sent_id), zi=~0, disp=~1
## final_recovery_model: keystrokes ~ condition * domain + scale(num_characters) + scale(num_minor) + , zi=~0, disp=~1
## final_recovery_model:     scale(num_major) + scale(MT_Quality) + scale(Difficulty) + , zi=~0, disp=~1
## final_recovery_model:     (1 | PET) + (1 | text_name/Sent_id), zi=~0, disp=~1
##                      Df    AIC    BIC  logLik deviance  Chisq Chi Df Pr(>Chisq)
## reduced_model        15 4483.1 4546.2 -2226.6   4453.1                         
## final_recovery_model 18 4487.6 4563.3 -2225.8   4451.6 1.5615      3     0.6682
# no significant differences in predictive power and AIC and BIC are lower, we keep the reduced

# Check Variance Inflation Factors
performance::check_collinearity(final_recovery_model) # since the full model has interaction terms. VIFs might be affected
## Model has interaction terms. VIFs might be inflated.
##   Try to center the variables used for the interaction, or check
##   multicollinearity among predictors of a model without interaction terms.
## # Check for Multicollinearity
## 
## Low Correlation
## 
##                   Term  VIF     VIF 95% CI adj. VIF Tolerance Tolerance 95% CI
##                 domain 1.33 [ 1.22,  1.51]     1.15      0.75     [0.66, 0.82]
##  scale(num_characters) 1.10 [ 1.04,  1.28]     1.05      0.91     [0.78, 0.97]
##       scale(num_minor) 2.34 [ 2.06,  2.70]     1.53      0.43     [0.37, 0.48]
##       scale(num_major) 1.61 [ 1.44,  1.83]     1.27      0.62     [0.55, 0.69]
##      scale(MT_Quality) 1.21 [ 1.12,  1.38]     1.10      0.82     [0.73, 0.89]
##      scale(Difficulty) 1.21 [ 1.11,  1.37]     1.10      0.83     [0.73, 0.90]
## 
## High Correlation
## 
##              Term   VIF     VIF 95% CI adj. VIF Tolerance Tolerance 95% CI
##         condition 16.79 [14.25, 19.82]     4.10      0.06     [0.05, 0.07]
##  condition:domain 11.57 [ 9.84, 13.63]     3.40      0.09     [0.07, 0.10]
performance::check_collinearity(reduced_model) # here no highly correlated variables
## # Check for Multicollinearity
## 
## Low Correlation
## 
##                   Term  VIF   VIF 95% CI adj. VIF Tolerance Tolerance 95% CI
##              condition 2.95 [2.57, 3.42]     1.72      0.34     [0.29, 0.39]
##                 domain 1.05 [1.01, 1.33]     1.02      0.95     [0.75, 0.99]
##  scale(num_characters) 1.09 [1.03, 1.28]     1.05      0.91     [0.78, 0.97]
##       scale(num_minor) 2.14 [1.89, 2.46]     1.46      0.47     [0.41, 0.53]
##       scale(num_major) 1.59 [1.43, 1.80]     1.26      0.63     [0.55, 0.70]
##      scale(MT_Quality) 1.18 [1.10, 1.35]     1.09      0.85     [0.74, 0.91]
##      scale(Difficulty) 1.17 [1.08, 1.33]     1.08      0.86     [0.75, 0.92]

Check stability

final_recovery_model$sdr$pdHess
## [1] TRUE
res <- simulateResiduals(reduced_model)
plot(res)

Further Simplification

summary(reduced_model)
##  Family: tweedie  ( log )
## Formula:          
## keystrokes ~ condition + domain + scale(num_characters) + scale(num_minor) +  
##     scale(num_major) + scale(MT_Quality) + scale(Difficulty) +  
##     (1 | PET) + (1 | text_name/Sent_id)
## Data: data
## 
##       AIC       BIC    logLik -2*log(L)  df.resid 
##    4483.1    4546.2   -2226.6    4453.1       481 
## 
## Random effects:
## 
## Conditional model:
##  Groups            Name        Variance Std.Dev.
##  PET               (Intercept) 0.24139  0.4913  
##  Sent_id:text_name (Intercept) 0.19421  0.4407  
##  text_name         (Intercept) 0.05942  0.2438  
## Number of obs: 496, groups:  PET, 8; Sent_id:text_name, 62; text_name, 8
## 
## Dispersion parameter for tweedie family (): 4.71 
## 
## Conditional model:
##                         Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)    
## (Intercept)            3.4374626  0.2467990  13.928  < 2e-16 ***
## condition2             0.0105001  0.1316736   0.080 0.936442    
## condition3             0.1644821  0.1225124   1.343 0.179410    
## condition4             0.0001308  0.1220912   0.001 0.999145    
## domainnews             0.0528307  0.2220476   0.238 0.811939    
## scale(num_characters)  0.3696635  0.0693018   5.334  9.6e-08 ***
## scale(num_minor)       0.0440478  0.0532688   0.827 0.408296    
## scale(num_major)       0.0680994  0.0424087   1.606 0.108320    
## scale(MT_Quality)     -0.1621306  0.0437090  -3.709 0.000208 ***
## scale(Difficulty)      0.1057136  0.0550186   1.921 0.054679 .  
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
reduced_model_noDiff <- update(reduced_model, . ~ . - scale(Difficulty))

res <- simulateResiduals(reduced_model_noDiff)
# Nella grafica base di R il titolo si assegna con 'main'
plot(res)

# now it works

summary(reduced_model_noDiff)
##  Family: tweedie  ( log )
## Formula:          
## keystrokes ~ condition + domain + scale(num_characters) + scale(num_minor) +  
##     scale(num_major) + scale(MT_Quality) + (1 | PET) + (1 | text_name/Sent_id)
## Data: data
## 
##       AIC       BIC    logLik -2*log(L)  df.resid 
##    4484.8    4543.7   -2228.4    4456.8       482 
## 
## Random effects:
## 
## Conditional model:
##  Groups            Name        Variance Std.Dev.
##  PET               (Intercept) 0.21555  0.4643  
##  Sent_id:text_name (Intercept) 0.19108  0.4371  
##  text_name         (Intercept) 0.05374  0.2318  
## Number of obs: 496, groups:  PET, 8; Sent_id:text_name, 62; text_name, 8
## 
## Dispersion parameter for tweedie family (): 4.72 
## 
## Conditional model:
##                       Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)    
## (Intercept)            3.37090    0.23460  14.369  < 2e-16 ***
## condition2             0.05010    0.13052   0.384 0.701088    
## condition3             0.19971    0.12176   1.640 0.100971    
## condition4             0.04567    0.12028   0.380 0.704166    
## domainnews             0.13091    0.21130   0.620 0.535580    
## scale(num_characters)  0.36379    0.06910   5.265  1.4e-07 ***
## scale(num_minor)       0.04600    0.05342   0.861 0.389122    
## scale(num_major)       0.06654    0.04252   1.565 0.117568    
## scale(MT_Quality)     -0.14641    0.04308  -3.398 0.000678 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
anova(reduced_model, reduced_model_noDiff)
## Data: data
## Models:
## reduced_model_noDiff: keystrokes ~ condition + domain + scale(num_characters) + scale(num_minor) + , zi=~0, disp=~1
## reduced_model_noDiff:     scale(num_major) + scale(MT_Quality) + (1 | PET) + (1 | text_name/Sent_id), zi=~0, disp=~1
## reduced_model: keystrokes ~ condition + domain + scale(num_characters) + scale(num_minor) + , zi=~0, disp=~1
## reduced_model:     scale(num_major) + scale(MT_Quality) + scale(Difficulty) + , zi=~0, disp=~1
## reduced_model:     (1 | PET) + (1 | text_name/Sent_id), zi=~0, disp=~1
##                      Df    AIC    BIC  logLik deviance  Chisq Chi Df Pr(>Chisq)
## reduced_model_noDiff 14 4484.8 4543.7 -2228.4   4456.8                         
## reduced_model        15 4483.1 4546.2 -2226.6   4453.1 3.6909      1    0.05471
##                       
## reduced_model_noDiff  
## reduced_model        .
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
# clear indication to stick with the second reduced model (no difficulty)

Plotting the regressors’ effects on keystrokes

# extract fixed effects table
coefs <- summary(reduced_model_noDiff)$coefficients$cond
df <- as.data.frame(coefs)

# add term names
df$term <- rownames(df)

# rename columns for convenience
df <- df %>%
  rename(
    estimate = Estimate,
    std.error = `Std. Error`,
    p.value = `Pr(>|z|)`
  )

# get confidence intervals
ci <- confint(reduced_model_noDiff, method = "Wald")

# keep only fixed effects
ci <- as.data.frame(ci)
ci$term <- rownames(ci)

df <- df %>%
  left_join(ci, by = "term") %>%
  rename(conf.low = `2.5 %`, conf.high = `97.5 %`)

# remove intercept
df <- df %>%
  filter(term != "(Intercept)")

# relabel terms
df <- df %>%
  mutate(
    term = recode(term,
                  "condition2" = "H-QE",
                  "condition3" = "H-APE",
                  "condition4" = "S-APE",
                  "domainnews" = "Domain: News",
                  "scale(num_characters)" = "Text length",
                  "scale(num_minor)" = "Minor errors",
                  "scale(num_major)" = "Major errors",
                  "scale(MT_Quality)" = "MT quality"
    ),
    significance = ifelse(p.value < 0.05, "Significant", "Not significant")
  )

df <- df %>%
  mutate(significant_flag = p.value < 0.05) %>%
  arrange(desc(significant_flag), desc(abs(estimate))) %>%
  mutate(term = factor(term, levels = rev(term)))
p <- ggplot(df, aes(x = estimate, y = term)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.6, color = "gray50") +
  
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = conf.low, xmax = conf.high),
                 height = 0.2, linewidth = 0.8, color = "gray40") +

  geom_point(aes(shape = as.factor(!significant_flag)),
             size = 3, stroke = 1.1, color = "black") +
  
  scale_shape_manual(
    name = NULL,              
    values = c(16, 1),        
    labels = c("Significant", "Not Significant") 
  ) +
  
  labs(
    title = "Effects on Keystrokes",
    subtitle = "Estimates with 95% confidence intervals (log scale)",
    x = "Effect size",
    y = NULL
  ) +
  
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(color = "gray30"),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 11),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 10),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_line(color = "gray85"),
    plot.margin = margin(10, 15, 10, 10),
    legend.position = "right"
  )

p

Random Intercepts plots

sjPlot::plot_model(reduced_model_noDiff, type = "re", grid = FALSE)[[2]]+
  theme_minimal()+
  labs(x = "Text", y= "Random Intercept", title = "Random Effects: Texts")

Comparing final model Vs other models

# Defining main formula
main_formula <- keystrokes ~ condition + domain + scale(num_characters) + 
  scale(num_minor) + scale(num_major) + scale(MT_Quality) + 
  (1 | PET) + (1 | text_name / Sent_id)

# Current Tweedie Model
mod_tweedie <- glmmTMB(main_formula, 
                       family = tweedie(link = "log"), 
                       data = data)


# Zero-Inflated Negative Binomial
# (Assumes some zeros come from a separate always-zero process)
mod_zinb <- glmmTMB(main_formula, 
                    ziformula = ~1, 
                    family = nbinom2, 
                    data = data)

# 3. Hurdle Model (Truncated Negative Binomial)
# (Assumes a process: 0 vs >0, then counts for the rest)
mod_hurdle <- glmmTMB(main_formula, 
                      ziformula = ~., 
                      family = truncated_nbinom2, 
                      data = data)
comparison <- compare_performance(mod_tweedie, mod_zinb, mod_hurdle, metrics = "common")
## Random effect variances not available. Returned R2 does not account for random effects.
print(comparison)
## # Comparison of Model Performance Indices
## 
## Name        |   Model |  AIC (weights) |  BIC (weights) | R2 (cond.)
## --------------------------------------------------------------------
## mod_tweedie | glmmTMB | 4484.8 (0.225) | 4543.7 (>.999) |      0.634
## mod_zinb    | glmmTMB | 4537.3 (<.001) | 4596.2 (<.001) |      0.966
## mod_hurdle  | glmmTMB | 4482.4 (0.775) | 4587.5 (<.001) |           
## 
## Name        | R2 (marg.) |   RMSE |   ICC
## -----------------------------------------
## mod_tweedie |      0.191 | 31.539 | 0.548
## mod_zinb    |      0.241 | 35.284 | 0.956
## mod_hurdle  |      0.263 | 34.485 |
# The 'rho' (power parameter)
glmmTMB::family_params(mod_tweedie)
## Tweedie power 
##      1.396782

Even though the Hurdle model has a slightly lower AIC, We would argue for the Tweedie model for three reasons: 1) The BIC weight for the Tweedie model is >.999. This is a massive statistical signal that the Tweedie model is the most parsimonious. 2) The Tweedie model has the lowest RMSE (31.539). This means its actual predictions are closer to actual data points than the other two models. 3) In the Tweedie model, the Itra Class Correlation (ICC) is 0.548, while in the ZI model, it’s 0.956, which is high and might suggests that the model is struggling to separate the random effects from the zero-inflation logic.
